subtopic: Evaluation and Management of Youth-Onset Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
question: Given the length of time since her original diagnosis, which investigation is most important next step in this patient's evaluation?
options: <Option B> Fasting and stimulated C-peptide measurements
explanation: The patient has had diabetes for three years without severe metabolic decompensation off treatment. The presence of positive antibodies, regardless of phenotype, is associated with more rapid progression to insulin requirement and patients with positive antibodies should be treated with insulin regardless of how they are doing on oral therapy.
reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: 'The presence of antibodies predicts rapid development of insulin requirement, as well as risk for development of other autoimmune disorders. Diabetes autoantibody testing also should also be considered in overweight/obese pubertal children with a clinical picture of T1DM (weight loss, ketosis/ketoacidosis), some of whom may have T2DM and be able to be weaned off of insulin for extended periods of time.'
title: YouthOnset Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
